paper_title: Geochemical and Hierarchical Cluster Analysis of Sediments

methods.description: This method involves analyzing the concentration of Total Organic Carbon (TOC) and Total Sulfur (TS) in sediment samples to understand the abundance and preservation of organic matter (OM) and infer environmental conditions during sediment deposition.

steps.description: Collect sediment samples from the T1 core at various intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from T1 core

steps.description: Measure TOC and TS concentrations in the collected sediment samples.
Analyze the relationship between TOC and TS values to infer depositional environment conditions.

methods.description: This method uses R-mode hierarchical cluster analysis to classify sediment samples into distinct palynofacies based on their composition, helping to interpret the depositional environment and its changes over time.

steps.description: Perform R-mode hierarchical cluster analysis using Ward's method with 1-Pearson distance.
Classify samples into four main clusters: Palynofacies A, B, C, and D.

methods.description: This method uses Q-mode hierarchical cluster analysis to identify associations of sediment samples, which are related to previously defined palynofacies, to further understand the depositional environment.

steps.description: Perform Q-mode hierarchical cluster analysis using Ward's method with Euclidean distance.
Identify five main associations of samples and relate them to palynofacies.
steps.searchable_summary: identify sample associations and relate to palynofacies
